Price when purchased online Overall pick Shoe Goo A ⚠ MCP client for `codex_apps` failed to start: MCP startup failed: handshaking with MCP server faile Codex '/compact' is disabled while a task is in progress. web agents workflow synthesis tutorials help pages executable web automation scripts web agents observation action abstraction DOM screenshot accessibility API comparison web agent failure modes taxonomy trajectory dataset WebArena Mind2Web BrowserGym agent authored web content authenticity provenance receipts C2PA delegated actions web agents agent hostile web bot detection automation fingerprinting AI crawlers measurement web agents human handoff protocol login 2FA approval resume task web agents semantic components action manifest schema.org aria machine readable actions web agents memory secrets privacy leakage reusable skills workflow memory Steven He Add your team Answer Links Images Share For a cholesterol/CVD evidence synthesis, give 3 recent top-tier sources on LDL/ApoB, insulin/high blood sugar, low-carb/keto claims, and atherosclerotic vessel blockage. Include citations and note uncertainty. Completed 3 steps Here are 3 recent, relatively top-tier sources that together cover LDL/ApoB, insulin/high blood sugar, low-carb/keto claims, and atherosclerotic blockage, with the main uncertainty noted. The strongest current synthesis is: ApoB is generally the better risk marker than LDL-C when they disagree, insulin resistance/high glucose track with atherosclerosis risk, and low-carb/keto claims depend heavily on diet quality and population studied. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih +2 1) ApoB / LDL-C National Lipid Association Expert Clinical Consensus on apoB in adult cardiovascular risk management, 2024. It states: “ApoB has been shown to be superior to LDL-C in risk assessment both before and during treatment with LLT” and that when LDL-C and apoB are discordant, risk “generally aligns better with apoB”. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih Why it matters: this is a recent, clinician-facing consensus from a major specialty society, so it is useful for evidence synthesis and current practice framing. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih Uncertainty: it is a consensus statement, not a new randomized outcomes trial; it reflects the field’s interpretation of the evidence rather than directly proving apoB causality by itself. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih 2) Insulin / high glucose Associations between insulin resistance indices and subclinical atherosclerosis, 2024 review/meta-level synthesis. It says, “Even in the absence of hyperglycemia or hyperlipidemia, it has been demonstrated that insulin resistance is an independent risk factor for atherosclerosis” and that the TyG index is the most consistent marker across studies. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih Why it matters: this directly connects insulin resistance/high blood sugar biology to early atherosclerosis measures such as CAC and arterial stiffness. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih Uncertainty: the paper is largely about associations and subclinical markers, so it supports risk linkage more than it proves that lowering insulin alone reduces hard cardiovascular events. pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih 3) Low-carb / keto claims and blockage Healthy Low-Carbohydrate Diets and Risk of Coronary Heart Disease, Harvard-led observational evidence summarized in 2026. It reports that healthier low-carb patterns were linked to lower CHD risk, while unhealthy low-carb patterns were linked to higher CHD risk; the same summary says the diet quality, not just carb restriction, drives the association. hsph.harvard +1 Keto-like diet observational study, 2023 ACC summary. It reports higher LDL-C and apoB and about a twofold higher risk of cardiovascular events, including “blocked arteries requiring stenting,” but also explicitly says the result is observational and cannot prove causality. acc Why it matters: this is the most relevant evidence bucket for the keto claim space, because it separates “low-carb” from “healthy low-carb” and shows that vascular outcomes can differ sharply by food quality. rheumatologyadvisor +1 Uncertainty: low-carb/keto evidence is heterogeneous; some short-term trials improve triglycerides and glycemia, while observational studies of high-animal-fat or low-quality patterns raise concern. That means broad “keto helps” or “keto harms” claims are both too strong. pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih +2 Practical synthesis Fact: atherosclerotic vessel blockage is driven by apoB-containing particles entering the arterial wall; LDL-C is an imperfect proxy, especially when metabolic syndrome or high triglycerides create discordance. newsroom.heart +1 Fact: insulin resistance and dysglycemia are repeatedly associated with atherosclerosis and plaque burden. academic.oup +1 Inference: for an evidence synthesis, the cleanest position is that apoB is usually the better lipid marker, insulin resistance is a meaningful upstream risk state, and low-carb/keto diets are not uniform—food quality and resulting apoB/LDL response matter more than the label. rheumatologyadvisor +2 41 sources Follow-ups Synthesis of ApoB/LDL and metabolic risk: interactive tool to track discordance in patient subgroups Computer ​ impact of saturated fat intake on LDL vs ApoB levels how to manage hyper-responders on ketogenic diets long-term cardiovascular outcomes of low-carbohydrate diets clinical utility of TyG index vs HbA1c in CVD risk prediction Get answers tailored to your health Unlock personalised insights and answers powered by your health data Visit Health Ask a follow-up Search Model
